repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
## Problem

Closes #34622.

Plugins that need the project root directory currently have two choices:
either depend on LSP being active and call `vim.lsp.get_clients()[1].root_dir`,
or re-implement root detection themselves with `vim.fs.root()`. There is no
shared, writable location where the project root can be stored once and read
by any plugin — LSP or not.

## Solution

Add a new buffer-local string option `'rootdir'` that any plugin can read or
write. The LSP client sets it automatically on attach (first-write-wins), so
it is populated for free when LSP is in use. Plugins that do not use LSP can
set it themselves via `vim.fs.root()` or any other detection strategy.

```lua
-- read project root from anywhere
local root = vim.bo.rootdir

-- set it without LSP (e.g. in a plugin)
vim.bo.rootdir = vim.fs.root(0, { '.git', 'Makefile' })
```

## Design decisions
Buffer-local, not global. Each buffer may belong to a different project
(e.g. two files open from different repos). A global option would be wrong.
Buffer-local matches LSP's own per-buffer attachment model.

First-write-wins in LSP. lsp._set_defaults() only writes rootdir when
it is still empty, following the same pattern used for tagfunc and omnifunc.
This means a plugin or user that sets rootdir before LSP attaches will not
have it overwritten. Subsequent LSP clients on the same buffer also do not
overwrite a value already set by the first client.

No cwd side effect. 'rootdir' is purely informational. It does not
change the working directory. The docs explicitly note this and point to
:lcd, :tcd, and getcwd() for that use case.

noglob flag. The value is a directory path that should never be
glob-expanded by option machinery.

Files changed
- src/nvim/options.lua — option metadata (type, scope, flags, default)
- src/nvim/buffer_defs.h — char *b_p_rootdir field in buf_T
- src/nvim/option_vars.h — EXTERN char *p_rootdir global default
- src/nvim/option.c — case kOptRootdir in get_option_var_for(); b_p_rootdir = empty_string_option in buf_copy_options()
- src/nvim/optionstr.c — check_string_option(&buf->b_p_rootdir) in check_buf_options()
- runtime/lua/vim/lsp.lua — set rootdir in lsp._set_defaults()
- runtime/doc/options.txt — :help 'rootdir' entry
- test/functional/plugin/lsp_spec.lua — three new tests

## Testing
Run all three new tests:

- Linux / macOS

```
TEST_FILE=test/functional/plugin/lsp_spec.lua TEST_FILTER='rootdir' make functionaltest
```

- Windows (Developer PowerShell for VS, with Developer Mode enabled)

```
$env:TEST_FILE = "test/functional/plugin/lsp_spec.lua"
$env:TEST_FILTER = "rootdir"
cmake --build build --target functionaltest
```

Test cases:

1. LSP sets rootdir when root_dir is provided and the option is empty
2. LSP does not overwrite rootdir already set before attach
3. rootdir stays empty when the client has no root_dir

Full functional test suite:

```
make functionaltest                        // Linux/macOS
cmake --build build --target functionaltest  // Windows
```

Doc validation:

```
make lintdoc                              // Linux/macOS
cmake --build build --target lintdoc      // Windows
```

comment_data.body: `b_p_rootdir` is a newly added allocated string option but it is not freed in `free_buf_options()` (src/nvim/buffer.c). This will leak memory when buffers are wiped/reinitialized. Add `clear_string_option(&buf->b_p_rootdir)` alongside the other buffer-local string options.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -600,6 +600,7 @@ struct file_buffer {
   int b_p_pi;                   ///< 'preserveindent'
   char *b_p_qe;                 ///< 'quoteescape'
   int b_p_ro;                   ///< 'readonly'
+  char *b_p_rootdir;            ///< 'rootdir'
   OptInt b_p_sw;                ///< 'shiftwidth'

comment_data.body: These tests wait only for `vim.lsp.buf_is_attached()`, but `buf_is_attached` becomes true before `client:on_attach()` runs (it sets `client.attached_buffers` first). Since `rootdir` is set during `_set_defaults()` on attach, this can race and make the test flaky. Consider waiting until `vim.bo[bufnr].rootdir` reaches the expected value (or until `on_attach` has run) instead of only waiting for `buf_is_attached`.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -574,6 +574,66 @@ describe('LSP', function()
       }
     end)
 
+    it('sets rootdir on attach when root_dir is provided', function()
+      exec_lua(create_server_definition)
+      local result = exec_lua(function()
+        local server = _G._create_server()
+        local bufnr = vim.api.nvim_create_buf(false, true)
+        vim.api.nvim_set_current_buf(bufnr)
+        local client_id = assert(vim.lsp.start({
+          name = 'rootdir-test',
+          cmd = server.cmd,
+          root_dir = '/my/project',
+        }))
+        local ok = vim.wait(1000, function()
+          return vim.lsp.buf_is_attached(bufnr, client_id)
+        end)
+        assert(ok, 'client did not attach')
+        return vim.bo[bufnr].rootdir
+      end)
+      eq('/my/project', result)

comment_data.body:
This generated meta file now contains an absolute, user-specific Windows path for the `backupdir` default (e.g. `C:/Users/Xing/...`). Committed generated files should not embed developer-machine paths; please regenerate in the standard docs/gen environment (or ensure defaults are rendered as portable placeholders like `$XDG_STATE_HOME`/`$LOCALAPPDATA`) so only the intended `rootdir` additions change.

This issue also appears in the following locations of the same file:
- line 1985
- line 2147
- line 6046
- line 7624
- line 7821
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -408,7 +408,7 @@ vim.go.bkc = vim.go.backupcopy
 --- uses another default.
 ---
 --- @type string
-vim.o.backupdir = ".,$XDG_STATE_HOME/nvim/backup//"
+vim.o.backupdir = ".,C:/Users/Xing/AppData/Local/nvim-data/backup//"

comment_data.body:
The help docs now show a user-specific Windows path for the default value of `'backupdir'` (includes a specific username). Help text should not embed developer-machine paths; please regenerate docs in the canonical environment or render portable placeholders (e.g. `$XDG_STATE_HOME`/`$LOCALAPPDATA`) so only the `rootdir` doc addition is included.

This issue also appears in the following locations of the same file:
- line 2341
- line 7052
- line 7225
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -970,7 +970,7 @@ A jump table for the options with a short description can be found at |Q_op|.
 	again not rename the file.
 
 						*'backupdir'* *'bdir'*
-'backupdir' 'bdir'	string	(default ".,$XDG_STATE_HOME/nvim/backup//")
+'backupdir' 'bdir'	string	(default ".,C:/Users/Xing/AppData/Local/nvim-data/backup//")

comment_data.body: Not a fan of how this depends on the order in which LSPs attach. I think ideally a `'rootdir'` option would be set some other way (perhaps its own global list of root markers?) and LSPs would use `'rootdir'` instead of their own `client.root_dir` if the option is set.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -876,6 +876,9 @@ function lsp._set_defaults(client, bufnr)
   if client:supports_method('textDocument/diagnostic') then
     lsp.diagnostic._enable(bufnr)
   end
+  if client.root_dir and vim.bo[bufnr].rootdir == '' then
+    vim.bo[bufnr].rootdir = client.root_dir
+  end

comment_data.body: imo this should also be able to be set globally (and also possibly tab locally?)
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -5102,6 +5102,23 @@ A jump table for the options with a short description can be found at |Q_op|.
 	This is useful for languages such as Hebrew, Arabic and Farsi.
 	The 'rightleft' option must be set for 'rightleftcmd' to take effect.
 
+							*'rootdir'*
+'rootdir'		string	(default "")
+			local to buffer

title: feat(options): add 'rootdir' buffer option to decouple project root from LSP
